description_long: This is a large, rectangular corrugated cardboard box, commonly used for shipping and transporting goods in urban environments. The box is sealed with clear packing tape along the top seam, and one side features a white barcode label with the code 'B1698' and two red upward-pointing arrows, indicating the correct upright orientation for handling. The cardboard surface shows subtle creases and tape marks, suggesting it has been handled or moved. The box is plain brown on most sides, with only one side displaying printed handling symbols and a barcode. Its size and construction suggest it could contain medium to large items, and it is likely filled with air or packing material around its contents. The overall appearance is utilitarian and typical for parcels found on sidewalks, in delivery trucks, or at building entrances.
description: A large, brown corrugated cardboard shipping box with a barcode label and handling arrows on one side, sealed with clear tape. The box is plain on most sides and shows minor creases and tape marks.
description_view_0: This view shows the side with a white barcode label ('B1698') and two red upward arrows, along with the taped seam on top.
description_view_1: This view displays a plain brown side of the box with visible creases and no labels or markings.
description_view_2: This view shows another side with two red upward arrows but no barcode label, and the taped seam is visible on top.
description_view_3: This view presents a plain brown side with no visible markings, labels, or arrows, and some subtle tape lines.
